{{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊}} * {{ill|Buddhist {{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | 名 = Shakyamuni Tathagata (釈迦如来) | 梵名 = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathagata
Shakyamuni World Honored One
Shakyamuni
Muni
Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | 画像 = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = {{ill|National Treasure|ja|国宝}} wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathagata statue ({{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺}}) | 経典 = {{ill|Lotus Sutra|ja|法華経}} | 信仰 = {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教}}
{{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教}} | 浄土 = {{ill|Vulture Peak|ja|霊鷲山}} Pure Land | See Also = {{ill|Prabhutaratna Buddha|ja|多宝如来}}
{{ill|Manjushri|ja|文殊菩薩}}
{{ill|Samantabhadra|ja|普賢菩薩}} }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教}}, Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教}}, Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|ja|ジャータカ}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara Buddha|ja|燃燈仏}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sutra|ja|方広大荘厳経}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tusita Heaven|ja|兜率天}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteshvara|ja|観音菩薩}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|ja|勢至菩薩}}. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | 名 = Shakyamuni Tathagata (釈迦如来) | 梵名 = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathagata
Shakyamuni World Honored One
Shakyamuni
Muni
Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | 画像 = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = {{ill|National Treasure|ja|国宝}} wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathagata statue ({{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺}}) | 経典 = {{ill|Lotus Sutra|ja|法華経}} | 信仰 = {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教}}
{{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教}} | 浄土 = {{ill|Vulture Peak|ja|霊鷲山}} Pure Land | See Also = {{ill|Prabhutaratna Buddha|ja|多宝如来}}
{{ill|Manjushri|ja|文殊菩薩}}
{{ill|Samantabhadra|ja|普賢菩薩}} }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教}}, Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教}}, Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|ja|ジャータカ}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara Buddha|ja|燃燈仏}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sutra|ja|方広大荘厳経}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tusita Heaven|ja|兜率天}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteshvara|ja|観音菩薩}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|ja|勢至菩薩}}. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | 画像 = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = {{ill|National Treasure|ja|国宝}} wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathagata statue ({{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺}}) | 経典 = {{ill|Lotus Sutra|ja|法華経}} | 信仰 = {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教}}
{{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教}} | 浄土 = {{ill|Vulture Peak|ja|霊鷲山}} Pure Land | See Also = {{ill|Prabhutaratna Buddha|ja|多宝如来}}
{{ill|Manjushri|ja|文殊菩薩}}
{{ill|Samantabhadra|ja|普賢菩薩}} }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教}}, Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教}}, Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|ja|ジャータカ}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara Buddha|ja|燃燈仏}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sutra|ja|方広大荘厳経}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tusita Heaven|ja|兜率天}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteshvara|ja|観音菩薩}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|ja|勢至菩薩}}. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] {{ill|Samantabhadra|ja|普賢菩薩}} }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教}}, Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教}}, Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|ja|ジャータカ}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara Buddha|ja|燃燈仏}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sutra|ja|方広大荘厳経}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tusita Heaven|ja|兜率天}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteshvara|ja|観音菩薩}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|ja|勢至菩薩}}. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] {{ill|Manjushri|ja|文殊菩薩}}
{{ill|Samantabhadra|ja|普賢菩薩}} }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教}}, Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教}}, Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|ja|ジャータカ}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara Buddha|ja|燃燈仏}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sutra|ja|方広大荘厳経}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tusita Heaven|ja|兜率天}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteshvara|ja|観音菩薩}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|ja|勢至菩薩}}. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] {{ill|Samantabhadra|ja|普賢菩薩}} }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教}}, Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教}}, Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|ja|ジャータカ}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara Buddha|ja|燃燈仏}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sutra|ja|方広大荘厳経}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tusita Heaven|ja|兜率天}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteshvara|ja|観音菩薩}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|ja|勢至菩薩}}. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:to be {{ill|Samantabhadra|ja|普賢菩薩}} }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教}}, Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教}}, Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|ja|ジャータカ}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara Buddha|ja|燃燈仏}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sutra|ja|方広大荘厳経}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tusita Heaven|ja|兜率天}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteshvara|ja|観音菩薩}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|ja|勢至菩薩}}. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利}} == References == {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:to [[大乗仏教]] |浄土=[[霊鷲山|霊山浄土]] |関連項目=[[多宝如来]]
[[文殊菩薩]]
[[普賢菩薩]] }} '''釈迦如来'''（しゃかにょらい）は、歴史的に実在した[[仏陀]]であり、[[仏教]]の開祖である[[釈迦]]（ゴータマ・シッダッタ、ゴータマ・シッダールタ、ガウタマ・シッダールタ、瞿曇悉達多）の[[如来]]としての名。釈迦牟尼仏、釈迦牟尼如来、釈迦牟尼世尊に同じ。 == 仏教史における釈迦如来 == === 上座部仏教 === [[上座部仏教]]では、理仏[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 『理仏性』 - コトバンク]ではない、歴史上に実在した[[釈迦]]、[[生身]]如来を指す。 === 大乗仏教 === [[大乗仏教]]では、[[三身]]説に説くように、理仏である[[法身]]仏が現世の人々の前に現れて教えを説いた姿、[[応身]]仏、あるいは[[報身]]仏であるとされている。 ==伝承による釈迦如来の過去世== ;スメーダ（『ブッダワンサ（仏種生経）』『ジャータカ』） :[[燃燈仏]]のもとで修行していた行者。[[燃燈仏]]から、未来に[[仏陀]]になると予言される。 ;浄幢菩薩（『ラリタヴィスタラ（[[方広大荘厳経]]）』） :スメーダが[[輪廻]]転生を繰り返し、[[兜率天]]に生まれた姿。釈迦の前世。 ;長邦（『観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経』） :[[バラモン教|バラモン]]僧。早離（そうり）、速離（そくり）という子がおり、それぞれ[[観音菩薩]]、[[勢至菩薩]]となる。 == 仏像としての釈迦如来 == {{For2|釈迦涅槃像|涅槃仏}} [[ファイル:悉曇梵字2.jpg|thumb|釈迦如来を表す[[梵字]]]] 釈迦如来は、[[インド]]以来、広く仏教の流布した地域で造像されるようになった。その中心は、実在の釈迦の伝記としての仏伝を絵解き風に造形化したもの、あるいは、その一場面を単独で造像したものなどであった。 日本では、誕生像、苦行像、降魔像、説法像、[[涅槃像]]などとして造像が行なわれた。なかでも説法像が一番一般的な造形であり、説法印などによって、釈迦が法を説く姿を表現している。 作例としては、奈良の[[法隆寺]]金堂、京都の[[蟹満寺]]の銅像、奈良の[[室生寺]]金堂、京都の[[大報恩寺]]の木像などが著名となっている。また、京都[[清凉寺]]の瑞像を模した清凉寺式釈迦如来も広範に流布している形式である。 '''[[釈迦三尊]]'''として祭壇に置かれる場合が多く、[[脇侍]]は[[文殊菩薩]]と[[普賢菩薩]]が多い。[[法華宗]]・[[日蓮宗]]では'''[[三宝尊]]（[[一塔両尊]]）'''の形式がとられることが多い。これは中心が[[題目]]の書かれた[[多宝塔]]（[[宝塔]]）で両脇に釈迦如来と[[多宝如来]]が祭祀者から見て左右に並び、その下に[[僧]]としての[[日蓮]]像がある。 === 主な釈迦如来像 === ; 国宝 * [[蟹満寺#文化財|銅造釈迦如来坐像]]（[[京都府]] [[木津川市]] [[蟹満寺]]本堂） * [[清涼寺#木造釈迦如来立像|木造釈迦如来立像]]（京都府 [[京都市]] [[清涼寺]]本堂） * 涅槃釈迦如来像（[[奈良県]] [[斑鳩町]] [[法隆寺]][[五重塔]]、塔本四面具涅槃塑像群の一つ） * [[法隆寺金堂釈迦三尊像|銅造釈迦如来及両脇侍像]]（奈良県 斑鳩町 法隆寺金堂） * [[法隆寺の仏像#上御堂の仏像|木造釈迦如来及両脇侍坐像]]（奈良県 斑鳩町 法隆寺上御堂） * [[東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|銅造誕生釈迦仏立像]]（奈良県 [[奈良市]] [[東大寺]] 東大寺ミュージアム） * [[室生寺#国宝|木造釈迦如来坐像]]（奈良県 [[宇陀市]] [[室生寺]]寶物殿） * [[室生寺#国宝|木造釈迦如来立像]]（奈良県 宇陀市 室生寺金堂） * [[深大寺#国宝|銅造釈迦如来倚像]] (「白鳳仏」、[[東京都]] [[調布市]] [[深大寺]]） *[[金剛峯寺#文化財（金剛峯寺所有）|涅槃図]]（[[和歌山県]] [[伊都郡]] [[高野山]] [[金剛峯寺]]　普段は[[高野山霊宝館]]に収蔵、[[涅槃会|常楽会]]で本尊として金剛峯寺広間に掛けられる。） ; 重要文化財・その他 * 釈迦如来像（和歌山県 伊都郡 高野山 真別処 円通律寺） *[[飛鳥寺#釈迦如来像（飛鳥大仏）|釈迦如来像]]（「飛鳥大仏」、奈良県 [[高市郡]] [[明日香村]] [[飛鳥寺]]） * [[建長寺#法堂|釈迦苦行像]]（[[神奈川県]][[鎌倉市]] [[建長寺]]法堂　[[パキスタン]]より寄贈） File:Shaka Nyorai.jpg|[[神護寺]]蔵 [[国宝絵画の一覧|国宝絵画]]「[[神護寺#国宝|絹本著色釈迦如来像]]」 File:Embroidery Sakyamuni.jpg|[[奈良国立博物館]]蔵 [[繡仏]]「刺繡釈迦如来説法図」 File:Seated Shaka Nyorai (Sakyamuni, Gautama Buddha).jpg|[[東京国立博物館]]蔵の[[平安時代]]釈迦如来坐像 File:Shaka Kami no Mido Horyuji.jpg|[[法隆寺]][[国宝彫刻の一覧|国宝彫刻]] [[法隆寺の仏像#上御堂の仏像|木造釈迦如来坐像]] == 舎利信仰 == 日本では、タイあるいはスリランカ政府より公式贈呈された遺骨が、[[浅草寺]]、[[道成寺]]、[[覚王山日泰寺]]、[[釈迦山大菩提寺]]に祀られている。 == フィクションにおける釈迦如来 == * [[西遊記]] - [[孫悟空]]を諌め、[[三蔵法師]]を[[天竺]]へ導く。 * [[蜘蛛の糸]] - [[芥川龍之介]]の短編小説。罪人のカンダタに極楽へ至る機会を与える。 == 釈迦如来にちなむ事物 == * 釈迦牟尼（にくるべ）という苗字がある。 == 関連項目 == {{columns-list|3| * [[釈迦三尊]] * [[三宝尊]]（[[一塔両尊]]） * [[釈迦院]] * [[釈迦堂]] * [[仏舎利]] * [[仏の一覧]] * [[三十二相八十種好]] * [[釈迦八相]][https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%87%88%E8%BF%A6%E5%85%AB%E7%9B%B8-524691　『釈迦八相』 - コトバンク] * [[理#仏教における「理」|仏教の「理」]] }} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{reflist}} {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} {{西遊記}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しやかによらい}} [[Category:釈迦|*によらい]] [[Category:如来]]

revision.text:
{{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | 名 = Shakyamuni Tathagata (釈迦如来) | 梵名 = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathagata
Shakyamuni World Honored One
Shakyamuni
Muni
Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | 画像 = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = {{ill|National Treasure|ja|国宝}} wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathagata statue ({{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺}}) | 経典 = {{ill|Lotus Sutra|ja|法華経}} | 信仰 = {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教}}
{{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教}} | 浄土 = {{ill|Vulture Peak|ja|霊鷲山}} Pure Land | See Also = {{ill|Prabhutaratna Buddha|ja|多宝如来}}
{{ill|Manjushri|ja|文殊菩薩}}
{{ill|Samantabhadra|ja|普賢菩薩}} }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教}}, Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教}}, Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|ja|ジャータカ}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara Buddha|ja|燃燈仏}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sutra|ja|方広大荘厳経}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tusita Heaven|ja|兜率天}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteshvara|ja|観音菩薩}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|ja|勢至菩薩}}. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利}} == References == {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:Śākyamuni]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Śākyamuni]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Śākyamuni]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Śākyamuni}}

Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺|12=draft}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=draft}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|12=draft}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺|12=draft}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像|12=draft}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|12=draft}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺|12=draft}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺|12=draft}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|12=draft}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記|12=draft}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|12=draft}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空|12=draft}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介|12=draft}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利|12=draft}} == References == {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:Śākyamuni]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Śākyamuni]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Śākyamuni]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Śākyamuni}}

{{ill|Samantabhadra|ja|普賢菩薩|12=draft}} }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺|12=draft}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=draft}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|12=draft}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺|12=draft}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像|12=draft}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|12=draft}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺|12=draft}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺|12=draft}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|12=draft}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記|12=draft}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|12=draft}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空|12=draft}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介|12=draft}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利|12=draft}} == References == {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:Śākyamuni]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Śākyamuni]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Śākyamuni]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Śākyamuni}}

{{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教|12=draft}} | 浄土 = {{ill|Vulture Peak|ja|霊鷲山|12=draft}} Pure Land | See Also = {{ill|Prabhutaratna Buddha|ja|多宝如来|12=draft}}
{{ill|Manjushri|ja|文殊菩薩|12=draft}}
{{ill|Samantabhadra|ja|普賢菩薩|12=draft}} }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教|12=draft}}, Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教|12=draft}}, Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|12=draft}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|ja|ジャータカ|12=draft}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara Buddha|ja|燃燈仏|12=draft}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sutra|ja|方広大荘厳経|12=draft}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tusita Heaven|ja|兜率天|12=draft}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|12=draft}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteshvara|ja|観音菩薩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|ja|勢至菩薩|12=draft}}. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺|12=draft}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=draft}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|12=draft}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺|12=draft}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像|12=draft}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|12=draft}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺|12=draft}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺|12=draft}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|12=draft}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記|12=draft}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|12=draft}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空|12=draft}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介|12=draft}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利|1=Śarīra|12=draft}} == References == {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Śākyamuni]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Śākyamuni]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Śākyamuni]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Śākyamuni}}

National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利|1=Śarīra|2=ja|3=仏舎利|4=de|5=Sarira|6=fr|7=Śarīra|1=Śarīra|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Śākyamuni]] }} [[sn:Śākyamuni]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Śākyamuni]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Śākyamuni}}

{{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | 名 = Shakyamuni Tathagata (釈迦如来) | 梵名 = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathagata
Shakyamuni World Honored One
Shakyamuni
Muni
Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | 画像 = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = {{ill|National Treasure|ja|国宝|12=draft}} wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathagata statue ({{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺|12=draft}}) | 経典 = {{ill|Lotus Sutra|ja|法華経|12=draft}} | 信仰 = {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教|12=draft}}
{{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教|12=draft}} | 浄土 = {{ill|Vulture Peak|ja|霊鷲山|12=draft}} Pure Land | See Also = {{ill|Prabhutaratna Buddha|ja|多宝如来|12=draft}}
{{ill|Manjushri|ja|文殊菩薩|12=draft}}
{{ill|Samantabhadra|ja|普賢菩薩|12=draft}} }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教|12=draft}}, Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教|12=draft}}, Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|12=draft}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|ja|ジャータカ|12=draft}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara Buddha|ja|燃燈仏|12=draft}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sutra|ja|方広大荘厳経|12=draft}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tusita Heaven|ja|兜率天|12=draft}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|12=draft}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteshvara|ja|観音菩薩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|ja|勢至菩薩|12=draft}}. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺|12=draft}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=draft}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|12=draft}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺|12=draft}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像|12=draft}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|12=draft}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺|12=draft}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺|12=draft}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|12=draft}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記|12=draft}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|12=draft}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空|12=draft}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介|12=draft}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利|1=Śarīra|2=ja|3=仏舎利|4=de|5=Sarira|6=fr|7=Śarīra|1=Śarīra|12=draft}} == References == {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Śākyamuni]] }} [[sn:Śākyamuni]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Śākyamuni]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Śākyamuni}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年9月26日 (火) 10:13 (UTC)}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha |名= 釈迦如来 |梵名= शाक्यमुनि |別名= 釈迦牟尼仏
釈迦牟尼如来
釈迦牟尼世尊
釈尊
牟尼
釈迦尊 |種字=[[File:Bhah.svg|25px]]　バク |真言・陀羅尼=ノウマクサンマンダ・ボダナン・バク |画像= [[ファイル:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] |説明文= [[国宝]]木造釈迦如来坐像（[[室生寺]]） |経典=『[[法華経]]』 |信仰=[[上座部仏教]]
[[大乗仏教]] |浄土=[[霊鷲山|霊山浄土]] |関連項目=[[多宝如来]]
[[文殊菩薩]]
[[普賢菩薩]] }} '''釈迦如来'''（しゃかにょらい）は、歴史的に実在した[[仏陀]]であり、[[仏教]]の開祖である[[釈迦]]（ゴータマ・シッダッタ、ゴータマ・シッダールタ、ガウタマ・シッダールタ、瞿曇悉達多）の[[如来]]としての名。釈迦牟尼仏、釈迦牟尼如来、釈迦牟尼世尊に同じ。 == 仏教史における釈迦如来 == === 上座部仏教 === [[上座部仏教]]では、理仏[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 『理仏性』 - コトバンク]ではない、歴史上に実在した[[釈迦]]、[[生身]]如来を指す。 === 大乗仏教 === [[大乗仏教]]では、[[三身]]説に説くように、理仏である[[法身]]仏が現世の人々の前に現れて教えを説いた姿、[[応身]]仏、あるいは[[報身]]仏であるとされている。 ==伝承による釈迦如来の過去世== ;スメーダ（『ブッダワンサ（仏種生経）』『ジャータカ』） :[[燃燈仏]]のもとで修行していた行者。[[燃燈仏]]から、未来に[[仏陀]]になると予言される。 ;浄幢菩薩（『ラリタヴィスタラ（[[方広大荘厳経]]）』） :スメーダが[[輪廻]]転生を繰り返し、[[兜率天]]に生まれた姿。釈迦の前世。 ;長邦（『観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経』） :[[バラモン教|バラモン]]僧。早離（そうり）、速離（そくり）という子がおり、それぞれ[[観音菩薩]]、[[勢至菩薩]]となる。 == 仏像としての釈迦如来 == {{For2|釈迦涅槃像|涅槃仏}} [[ファイル:悉曇梵字2.jpg|thumb|釈迦如来を表す[[梵字]]]] 釈迦如来は、[[インド]]以来、広く仏教の流布した地域で造像されるようになった。その中心は、実在の釈迦の伝記としての仏伝を絵解き風に造形化したもの、あるいは、その一場面を単独で造像したものなどであった。 日本では、誕生像、苦行像、降魔像、説法像、[[涅槃像]]などとして造像が行なわれた。なかでも説法像が一番一般的な造形であり、説法印などによって、釈迦が法を説く姿を表現している。 作例としては、奈良の[[法隆寺]]金堂、京都の[[蟹満寺]]の銅像、奈良の[[室生寺]]金堂、京都の[[大報恩寺]]の木像などが著名となっている。また、京都[[清凉寺]]の瑞像を模した清凉寺式釈迦如来も広範に流布している形式である。 '''[[釈迦三尊]]'''として祭壇に置かれる場合が多く、[[脇侍]]は[[文殊菩薩]]と[[普賢菩薩]]が多い。[[法華宗]]・[[日蓮宗]]では'''[[三宝尊]]（[[一塔両尊]]）'''の形式がとられることが多い。これは中心が[[題目]]の書かれた[[多宝塔]]（[[宝塔]]）で両脇に釈迦如来と[[多宝如来]]が祭祀者から見て左右に並び、その下に[[僧]]としての[[日蓮]]像がある。 === 主な釈迦如来像 === ; 国宝 * [[蟹満寺#文化財|銅造釈迦如来坐像]]（[[京都府]] [[木津川市]] [[蟹満寺]]本堂） * [[清涼寺#木造釈迦如来立像|木造釈迦如来立像]]（京都府 [[京都市]] [[清涼寺]]本堂） * 涅槃釈迦如来像（[[奈良県]] [[斑鳩町]] [[法隆寺]][[五重塔]]、塔本四面具涅槃塑像群の一つ） * [[法隆寺金堂釈迦三尊像|銅造釈迦如来及両脇侍像]]（奈良県 斑鳩町 法隆寺金堂） * [[法隆寺の仏像#上御堂の仏像|木造釈迦如来及両脇侍坐像]]（奈良県 斑鳩町 法隆寺上御堂） * [[東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|銅造誕生釈迦仏立像]]（奈良県 [[奈良市]] [[東大寺]] 東大寺ミュージアム） * [[室生寺#国宝|木造釈迦如来坐像]]（奈良県 [[宇陀市]] [[室生寺]]寶物殿） * [[室生寺#国宝|木造釈迦如来立像]]（奈良県 宇陀市 室生寺金堂） * [[深大寺#国宝|銅造釈迦如来倚像]] (「白鳳仏」、[[東京都]] [[調布市]] [[深大寺]]） *[[金剛峯寺#文化財（金剛峯寺所有）|涅槃図]]（[[和歌山県]] [[伊都郡]] [[高野山]] [[金剛峯寺]]　普段は[[高野山霊宝館]]に収蔵、[[涅槃会|常楽会]]で本尊として金剛峯寺広間に掛けられる。） ; 重要文化財・その他 * 釈迦如来像（和歌山県 伊都郡 高野山 真別処 円通律寺） *[[飛鳥寺#釈迦如来像（飛鳥大仏）|釈迦如来像]]（「飛鳥大仏」、奈良県 [[高市郡]] [[明日香村]] [[飛鳥寺]]） * [[建長寺#法堂|釈迦苦行像]]（[[神奈川県]][[鎌倉市]] [[建長寺]]法堂　[[パキスタン]]より寄贈） File:Shaka Nyorai.jpg|[[神護寺]]蔵 [[国宝絵画の一覧|国宝絵画]]「[[神護寺#国宝|絹本著色釈迦如来像]]」 File:Embroidery Sakyamuni.jpg|[[奈良国立博物館]]蔵 [[繡仏]]「刺繡釈迦如来説法図」 File:Seated Shaka Nyorai (Sakyamuni, Gautama Buddha).jpg|[[東京国立博物館]]蔵の[[平安時代]]釈迦如来坐像 File:Shaka Kami no Mido Horyuji.jpg|[[法隆寺]][[国宝彫刻の一覧|国宝彫刻]] [[法隆寺の仏像#上御堂の仏像|木造釈迦如来坐像]] == 舎利信仰 == 日本では、タイあるいはスリランカ政府より公式贈呈された遺骨が、[[浅草寺]]、[[道成寺]]、[[覚王山日泰寺]]、[[釈迦山大菩提寺]]に祀られている。 == フィクションにおける釈迦如来 == * [[西遊記]] - [[孫悟空]]を諌め、[[三蔵法師]]を[[天竺]]へ導く。 * [[蜘蛛の糸]] - [[芥川龍之介]]の短編小説。罪人のカンダタに極楽へ至る機会を与える。 == 釈迦如来にちなむ事物 == * 釈迦牟尼（にくるべ）という苗字がある。 == 関連項目 == {{columns-list|3| * [[釈迦三尊]] * [[三宝尊]]（[[一塔両尊]]） * [[釈迦院]] * [[釈迦堂]] * [[仏舎利]] * [[仏の一覧]] * [[三十二相八十種好]] * [[釈迦八相]][https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%87%88%E8%BF%A6%E5%85%AB%E7%9B%B8-524691　『釈迦八相』 - コトバンク] * [[理#仏教における「理」|仏教の「理」]] }} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{reflist}} {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} {{西遊記}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しやかによらい}} [[Category:釈迦|*によらい]] [[Category:如来]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年9月26日 (火) 10:13 (UTC)}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha |名= 釈迦如来 |梵名= शाक्यमुनि |別名= 釈迦牟尼仏
釈迦牟尼如来
釈迦牟尼世尊
釈尊
牟尼
釈迦尊 |種字=[[File:Bhah.svg|25px]]　バク |真言・陀羅尼=ノウマクサンマンダ・ボダナン・バク |画像= [[ファイル:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] |説明文= [[国宝]]木造釈迦如来坐像（[[室生寺]]） |経典=『[[法華経]]』 |信仰=[[上座部仏教]]
[[大乗仏教]] |浄土=[[霊鷲山|霊山浄土]] |関連項目=[[多宝如来]]
[[文殊菩薩]]
[[普賢菩薩]] }} '''釈迦如来'''（しゃかにょらい）は、歴史的に実在した[[仏陀]]であり、[[仏教]]の開祖である[[釈迦]]（ゴータマ・シッダッタ、ゴータマ・シッダールタ、ガウタマ・シッダールタ、瞿曇悉達多）の[[如来]]としての名。釈迦牟尼仏、釈迦牟尼如来、釈迦牟尼世尊に同じ。 == 仏教史における釈迦如来 == === 上座部仏教 === [[上座部仏教]]では、理仏[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 『理仏性』 - コトバンク]ではない、歴史上に実在した[[釈迦]]、[[生身]]如来を指す。 === 大乗仏教 === [[大乗仏教]]では、[[三身]]説に説くように、理仏である[[法身]]仏が現世の人々の前に現れて教えを説いた姿、[[応身]]仏、あるいは[[報身]]仏であるとされている。 ==伝承による釈迦如来の過去世== ;スメーダ（『ブッダワンサ（仏種生経）』『ジャータカ』） :[[燃燈仏]]のもとで修行していた行者。[[燃燈仏]]から、未来に[[仏陀]]になると予言される。 ;浄幢菩薩（『ラリタヴィスタラ（[[方広大荘厳経]]）』） :スメーダが[[輪廻]]転生を繰り返し、[[兜率天]]に生まれた姿。釈迦の前世。 ;長邦（『観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経』） :[[バラモン教|バラモン]]僧。早離（そうり）、速離（そくり）という子がおり、それぞれ[[観音菩薩]]、[[勢至菩薩]]となる。 == 仏像としての釈迦如来 == {{For2|釈迦涅槃像|涅槃仏}} [[ファイル:悉曇梵字2.jpg|thumb|釈迦如来を表す[[梵字]]]] 釈迦如来は、[[インド]]以来、広く仏教の流布した地域で造像されるようになった。その中心は、実在の釈迦の伝記としての仏伝を絵解き風に造形化したもの、あるいは、その一場面を単独で造像したものなどであった。 日本では、誕生像、苦行像、降魔像、説法像、[[涅槃像]]などとして造像が行なわれた。なかでも説法像が一番一般的な造形であり、説法印などによって、釈迦が法を説く姿を表現している。 作例としては、奈良の[[法隆寺]]金堂、京都の[[蟹満寺]]の銅像、奈良の[[室生寺]]金堂、京都の[[大報恩寺]]の木像などが著名となっている。また、京都[[清凉寺]]の瑞像を模した清凉寺式釈迦如来も広範に流布している形式である。 '''[[釈迦三尊]]'''として祭壇に置かれる場合が多く、[[脇侍]]は[[文殊菩薩]]と[[普賢菩薩]]が多い。[[法華宗]]・[[日蓮宗]]では'''[[三宝尊]]（[[一塔両尊]]）'''の形式がとられることが多い。これは中心が[[題目]]の書かれた[[多宝塔]]（[[宝塔]]）で両脇に釈迦如来と[[多宝如来]]が祭祀者から見て左右に並び、その下に[[僧]]としての[[日蓮]]像がある。 === 主な釈迦如来像 === ; 国宝 * [[蟹満寺#文化財|銅造釈迦如来坐像]]（[[京都府]] [[木津川市]] [[蟹満寺]]本堂） * [[清涼寺#木造釈迦如来立像|木造釈迦如来立像]]（京都府 [[京都市]] [[清涼寺]]本堂） * 涅槃釈迦如来像（[[奈良県]] [[斑鳩町]] [[法隆寺]][[五重塔]]、塔本四面具涅槃塑像群の一つ） * [[法隆寺金堂釈迦三尊像|銅造釈迦如来及両脇侍像]]（奈良県 斑鳩町 法隆寺金堂） * [[法隆寺の仏像#上御堂の仏像|木造釈迦如来及両脇侍坐像]]（奈良県 斑鳩町 法隆寺上御堂） * [[東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|銅造誕生釈迦仏立像]]（奈良県 [[奈良市]] [[東大寺]] 東大寺ミュージアム） * [[室生寺#国宝|木造釈迦如来坐像]]（奈良県 [[宇陀市]] [[室生寺]]寶物殿） * [[室生寺#国宝|木造釈迦如来立像]]（奈良県 宇陀市 室生寺金堂） * [[深大寺#国宝|銅造釈迦如来倚像]] (「白鳳仏」、[[東京都]] [[調布市]] [[深大寺]]） *[[金剛峯寺#文化財（金剛峯寺所有）|涅槃図]]（[[和歌山県]] [[伊都郡]] [[高野山]] [[金剛峯寺]]　普段は[[高野山霊宝館]]に収蔵、[[涅槃会|常楽会]]で本尊として金剛峯寺広間に掛けられる。） ; 重要文化財・その他 * 釈迦如来像（和歌山県 伊都郡 高野山 真別処 円通律寺） *[[飛鳥寺#釈迦如来像（飛鳥大仏）|釈迦如来像]]（「飛鳥大仏」、奈良県 [[高市郡]] [[明日香村]] [[飛鳥寺]]） * [[建長寺#法堂|釈迦苦行像]]（[[神奈川県]][[鎌倉市]] [[建長寺]]法堂　[[パキスタン]]より寄贈） File:Shaka Nyorai.jpg|[[神護寺]]蔵 [[国宝絵画の一覧|国宝絵画]]「[[神護寺#国宝|絹本著色釈迦如来像]]」 File:Embroidery Sakyamuni.jpg|[[奈良国立博物館]]蔵 [[繡仏]]「刺繡釈迦如来説法図」 File:Seated Shaka Nyorai (Sakyamuni, Gautama Buddha).jpg|[[東京国立博物館]]蔵の[[平安時代]]釈迦如来坐像 File:Shaka Kami no Mido Horyuji.jpg|[[法隆寺]][[国宝彫刻の一覧|国宝彫刻]] [[法隆寺の仏像#上御堂の仏像|木造釈迦如来坐像]] == 舎利信仰 == 日本では、タイあるいはスリランカ政府より公式贈呈された遺骨が、[[浅草寺]]、[[道成寺]]、[[覚王山日泰寺]]、[[釈迦山大菩提寺]]に祀られている。 == フィクションにおける釈迦如来 == * [[西遊記]] - [[孫悟空]]を諌め、[[三蔵法師]]を[[天竺]]へ導く。 * [[蜘蛛の糸]] - [[芥川龍之介]]の短編小説。罪人のカンダタに極楽へ至る機会を与える。 == 釈迦如来にちなむ事物 == * 釈迦牟尼（にくるべ）という苗字がある。 == 関連項目 == {{columns-list|3| * [[釈迦三尊]] * [[三宝尊]]（[[一塔両尊]]） * [[釈迦院]] * [[釈迦堂]] * [[仏舎利]] * [[仏の一覧]] * [[三十二相八十種好]] * [[釈迦八相]][https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%87%88%E8%BF%A6%E5%85%AB%E7%9B%B8-524691　『釈迦八相』 - コトバンク] * [[理#仏教における「理」|仏教の「理」]] }} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{reflist}} {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} {{西遊記}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しやかによらい}} [[Category:釈迦|*によらい]] [[Category:如来]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Śākyamuni]] to [[Shakyamuni]]: better spelling

revision.text:
{{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | 名 = Shakyamuni Tathagata (釈迦如来) | 梵名 = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathagata
Shakyamuni World Honored One
Shakyamuni
Muni
Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | 画像 = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = {{ill|National Treasure|ja|国宝|qq=draft}} wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathagata statue ({{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺|qq=draft}}) | 経典 = {{ill|Lotus Sutra|ja|法華経|qq=draft}} | 信仰 = {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教|qq=draft}} | 浄土 = {{ill|Vulture Peak|ja|霊鷲山|qq=draft}} Pure Land | See Also = {{ill|Prabhutaratna Buddha|ja|多宝如来|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Manjushri|ja|文殊菩薩|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Samantabhadra|ja|普賢菩薩|qq=draft}} }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教|qq=draft}}, Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教|qq=draft}}, Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|qq=draft}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|ja|ジャータカ|qq=draft}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara Buddha|ja|燃燈仏|qq=draft}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sutra|ja|方広大荘厳経|qq=draft}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tusita Heaven|ja|兜率天|qq=draft}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteshvara|ja|観音菩薩|1=Guanyin|2=ja|3=観音菩薩|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|ja|勢至菩薩|qq=draft}}. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺|qq=draft}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺|qq=draft}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺|qq=draft}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記|qq=draft}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空|qq=draft}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介|qq=draft}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利|1=Śarīra|2=ja|3=仏舎利|4=de|5=Sarira|6=fr|7=Śarīra|1=Śarīra|qq=draft}} == References == {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shakyamuni}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shakyamuni]] }} [[sn:Shakyamuni]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Shakyamuni]]

Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | 画像 = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = {{ill|National Treasure|ja|国宝|qq=draft}} wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathagata statue ({{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺|qq=draft}}) | 経典 = {{ill|Lotus Sutra|ja|法華経|qq=draft}} | 信仰 = {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教|qq=draft}} | 浄土 = {{ill|Vulture Peak|ja|霊鷲山|qq=draft}} Pure Land | See Also = {{ill|Prabhutaratna Buddha|ja|多宝如来|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Manjushri|ja|文殊菩薩|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Samantabhadra|ja|普賢菩薩|qq=draft}} }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教|qq=draft}}, Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教|qq=draft}}, Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|qq=draft}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|ja|ジャータカ|qq=draft}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara Buddha|ja|燃燈仏|qq=draft}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sutra|ja|方広大荘厳経|qq=draft}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tusita Heaven|ja|兜率天|qq=draft}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteshvara|ja|観音菩薩|1=Guanyin|2=ja|3=観音菩薩|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|ja|勢至菩薩|qq=draft}}. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺|qq=draft}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺|qq=draft}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺|qq=draft}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記|qq=draft}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空|qq=draft}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介|qq=draft}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利|1=Śarīra|2=ja|3=仏舎利|4=de|5=Sarira|6=fr|7=Śarīra|1=Śarīra|qq=draft}} == References == {{Buddhism2}} {{仏陀}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shakyamuni]] }} [[sn:Shakyamuni]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Shakyamuni]]

{{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | 名 = Shakyamuni Tathagata (釈迦如来) | 梵名 = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathagata
Shakyamuni World Honored One
Shakyamuni
Muni
Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | 画像 = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = {{ill|National Treasure|ja|国宝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Treasure|qq=}} wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathagata statue ({{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroji Temple|qq=}}) | 経典 = {{ill|Lotus Sutra|ja|法華経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lotus Sutra|qq=}} | 信仰 = {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theravada Buddhism|qq=}}
{{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana Buddhism|qq=}} | 浄土 = {{ill|Vulture Peak|ja|霊鷲山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vulture Peak|qq=}} Pure Land | See Also = {{ill|Prabhutaratna Buddha|ja|多宝如来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prabhutaratna Buddha|qq=}}
{{ill|Manjushri|ja|文殊菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjushri|qq=}}
{{ill|Samantabhadra|ja|普賢菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samantabhadra|qq=}} }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theravada Buddhism|qq=}}, Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana Buddhism|qq=}}, Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|qq=}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|ja|ジャータカ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jataka tales|qq=}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara Buddha|ja|燃燈仏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dipankara Buddha|qq=}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sutra|ja|方広大荘厳経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lalitavistara Sutra|qq=}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tusita Heaven|ja|兜率天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tusita Heaven|qq=}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|qq=}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteshvara|ja|観音菩薩|1=Kannon|lt=Guanyin|2=ja|3=観音菩薩|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokiteshvara|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanyin|qq=}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|ja|勢至菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahasthamaprapta|qq=}}. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple|qq=}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroji Temple|qq=}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryoji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni|qq=}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|qq=}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|qq=}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todaiji Temple|qq=}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sensoji Temple|qq=}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dojoji Temple|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Nittai-ji|ja|覚王山日泰寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakuozan Nittai-ji|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|ja|西遊記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Journey to the West|qq=}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|qq=}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Wukong|qq=}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryunosuke Akutagawa|qq=}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Trinity|qq=}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|qq=}} * {{ill|Buddhist Relics|ja|仏舎利|1=Śarīra|2=ja|3=仏舎利|4=de|5=Sarira|6=fr|7=Śarīra|1=Śarīra|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist Relics|13=User:Immanuelle/Śarīra|qq=}} == References == {{仏陀}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shakyamuni Buddha]] [[Category:Tathagata]] [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shakyamuni]] }} [[sn:Shakyamuni]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Shakyamuni]]

Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroji Temple|qq=}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryoji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Temple|ja|東大寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todaiji Temple|qq=}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Sanzang|qq=}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Wukong|qq=}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke Akutagawa|ja|芥川龍之介|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryunosuke Akutagawa|qq=}}: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni {{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | 名 = Shakyamuni Tathagata (釈迦如来) | 梵名 = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Buddha

Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | 画像 = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = {{ill|National Treasure|ja|国宝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Treasure|qq=}} wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathagata statue ({{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroji Temple|qq=}}) | 経典 = {{ill|Lotus Sutra|ja|法華経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lotus Sutra|qq=}} | 信仰 = {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theravada Buddhism|qq=}}
{{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana Buddhism|qq=}} | 浄土 = {{ill|Vulture Peak|ja|霊鷲山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vulture Peak|qq=}} Pure Land | See Also = {{ill|Prabhutaratna Buddha|ja|多宝如来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prabhutaratna Buddha|qq=}}
{{ill|Manjushri|ja|文殊菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjushri|qq=}}
{{ill|Samantabhadra|ja|普賢菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samantabhadra|qq=}} }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada Buddhism|ja|上座部仏教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theravada Buddhism|qq=}}, Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana Buddhism|ja|大乗仏教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana Buddhism|qq=}}, Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|qq=}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|ja|ジャータカ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jataka tales|qq=}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara Buddha|ja|燃燈仏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dipankara Buddha|qq=}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sutra|ja|方広大荘厳経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lalitavistara Sutra|qq=}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tusita Heaven|ja|兜率天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tusita Heaven|qq=}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|qq=}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteshvara|ja|観音菩薩|1=Kannon|lt=Guanyin|2=ja|3=観音菩薩|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokiteshvara|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanyin|qq=}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|ja|勢至菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahasthamaprapta|qq=}}. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Horyuji Temple|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple|qq=}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, and {{ill|Muroji Temple|ja|室生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroji Temple|qq=}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ({{ill|Seiryoji Temple|ja|清凉寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryoji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni|qq=}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|qq=}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|qq=}} ({{ill|Todaiji Temple|ja|東大寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todaiji Temple|qq=}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like {{ill|Sensoji Temple|ja|浅草寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sensoji Temple|qq=}}, {{ill|Dojoji Temple|ja|道成寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dojoji Temple|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakuozan Sanzang|qq=}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|ja|孫悟空|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Wukong|qq=}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryunosuke
