He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|qq=}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|qq=}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni|qq=}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|qq=}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|qq=}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|qq=}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Trinity|qq=}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|qq=}} * [[:en:Śarīra|Śarīra]] == References == {{仏陀}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Shakyamuni]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Shakyamuni]]

In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni|qq=}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|qq=}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|qq=}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|qq=}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Trinity|qq=}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|qq=}} * [[:en:Śarīra|Śarīra]] == References == {{仏陀}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Shakyamuni]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Shakyamuni]] [[Category:Buddhas]]

{{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | 名 = Shakyamuni Tathagata (釈迦如来) | 梵名 = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathagata
Shakyamuni World Honored One
Shakyamuni
Muni
Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | 画像 = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathagata statue ([[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]) | 経典 = [[:en:Lotus Sutra|Lotus Sutra]] | 信仰 = [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]]
[[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]] | 浄土 = [[:en:Vulture Peak|Vulture Peak]] Pure Land | See Also = [[:en:Prabhutaratna|Prabhutaratna Buddha]]
[[:en:Manjushri|Manjushri]]
[[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[Buddha]] and founder of [[Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|qq=}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|qq=}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni|qq=}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|qq=}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|qq=}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|qq=}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Trinity|qq=}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|qq=}} * [[:en:Śarīra|Śarīra]] == References == {{仏陀}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist Art]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Shakyamuni]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Shakyamuni]] [[Category:Buddhas]] [[Category:Gautama Buddha]]

He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[Buddhism]] since its origin in [[India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|qq=}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|qq=}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Trinity|qq=}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|qq=}} * [[:en:Śarīra|Śarīra]] == References == {{仏陀}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Shakyamuni]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Shakyamuni]] [[Category:Buddhas]] [[Category:Gautama Buddha]]

Shakyamuni in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|qq=}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Trinity|qq=}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|qq=}} * [[:en:Śarīra|Śarīra]] == References == {{仏陀}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft {{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | 名 = Shakyamuni Tathagata (釈迦如来) | 梵名 = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathagata
Shakyamuni World Honored One
Shakyamuni
Muni
Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | 画像 = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathagata statue ([[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]) | 経典 = [[:en:Lotus Sutra|Lotus Sutra]] | 信仰 = [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]]
[[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]] | 浄土 = [[:en:Vulture Peak|Vulture Peak]] Pure Land | See Also = [[:en:Prabhutaratna|Prabhutaratna Buddha]]
[[:en:Manjushri|Manjushri]]
[[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] }} '''Shakyamuni Tathagata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[:en:The Buddha|The Buddha]] and founder of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[Nirmanakaya]] or [[Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] since its origin in [[:en:India|India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|qq=}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|qq=}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Trinity|qq=}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|qq=}} * [[:en:Śarīra|Śarīra]] == References == {{仏陀}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Shakyamuni]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Shakyamuni]] [[Category:Buddhas]] [[Category:Gautama Buddha]]

He is also called Shakyamuni Buddha, Shakyamuni World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni. == Shakyamuni Tathagata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathagata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[:en:Nirmanakaya|Nirmanakaya]] or [[:en:Sambhogakaya|Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathagata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Siddham Bija Shaka.jpg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathagata represented by the [[:en:Siddham|Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni have been created throughout regions influenced by [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] since its origin in [[:en:India|India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathagata|qq=}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni and Ritual Bowl|qq=}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=}}. == Shakyamuni in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|qq=}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Trinity|qq=}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|qq=}} * [[:en:Śarīra|Śarīra]] == References == {{仏陀}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Shakyamuni]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Shakyamuni]] [[Category:Buddhas]] [[Category:Gautama Buddha]]

Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata Tathagata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata Tathagata|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata Tathagata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata Tathagata|qq=}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|qq=}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|qq=}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|qq=}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|qq=}} * [[:en:Śarīra|Śarīra]] == References == {{仏陀}} {{translated revision.comment: Text replacement - "Shakyamuni Tathāgata Tathagata" to "Shakyamuni Tathāgata"
revision.text:
{{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | 名 = Shakyamuni Tathāgata (釈迦如来) | 梵名 = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathāgata
Shakyamuni Tathāgata World Honored One
Shakyamuni Tathāgata
Muni
Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | 画像 = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata statue ([[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]) | 経典 = [[:en:Lotus Sutra|Lotus Sutra]] | 信仰 = [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]]
[[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]] | 浄土 = [[:en:Vulture Peak|Vulture Peak]] Pure Land | See Also = [[:en:Prabhutaratna|Prabhutaratna Buddha]]
[[:en:Manjushri|Manjushri]]
[[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] }} '''Shakyamuni Tathāgata''' (釈迦如来, Shaka Nyorai) refers to the historical [[:en:The Buddha|The Buddha]] and founder of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], [[Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] (Gautama Siddhartha, Siddhattha Gotama, Siddhartha Gautama, 瞿曇悉達多), in his aspect as a Tathagata. He is also called Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha, Shakyamuni Tathāgata World Honored One, and simply Shakyamuni Tathāgata. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[:en:Nirmanakaya|Nirmanakaya]] or [[:en:Sambhogakaya|Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == {{For2|Shakyamuni Tathāgata reclining statues|涅槃仏}} [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the [[:en:Siddham|Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] since its origin in [[:en:India|India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|qq=}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|qq=}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|qq=}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|qq=}} * [[:en:Śarīra|Śarīra]] == References == {{仏陀}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] [[Category:Buddhas]] [[Category:Gautama Buddha]]

[[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]] | 浄土 = [[:en:Vulture Peak|Vulture Peak]] Pure Land | See Also = [[:en:Prabhutaratna|Prabhutaratna Buddha]]

{{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | 名 = Shakyamuni Tathāgata (釈迦如来) | 梵名 = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathāgata
Shakyamuni Tathāgata World Honored One
Shakyamuni Tathāgata
Muni
Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | 画像 = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata statue ([[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]) | 経典 = [[:en:Lotus Sutra|Lotus Sutra]] | 信仰 = [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]]
[[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]] | 浄土 = [[:en:Vulture Peak|Vulture Peak]] Pure Land | See Also = [[:en:Prabhutaratna|Prabhutaratna Buddha]]
[[:en:Manjushri|Manjushri]]
[[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shakyamuni Tathāgata'''|釈迦如来|Shaka Nyorai}} refers to the historical [[:en:The Buddha|Buddha]] and founder of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], {{nihongo|'''Siddhartha Gautama'''|瞿曇悉達多|Kudon Shiddata|10=くどんしっだった}}, in his aspect as a [[Tathāgata]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[:en:Nirmanakaya|Nirmanakaya]] or [[:en:Sambhogakaya|Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the [[:en:Siddham|Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] since its origin in [[:en:India|India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|qq=}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|qq=}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|qq=}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|qq=}} * [[:en:Śarīra|Śarīra]] == References == {{仏陀}} {{translated Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the [[:en:Siddham|Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] since its origin in [[:en:India|India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|qq=}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|qq=}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|qq=}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|qq=}} * [[:en:Śarīra|Śarīra]] == References == {{仏陀}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] [[Category:Buddhas]] [[Category:Gautama Buddha]]

[[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shakyamuni Tathāgata'''|釈迦如来|Shaka Nyorai}} refers to the historical [[:en:The Buddha|Buddha]] and founder of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], {{nihongo|'''Siddhartha Gautama'''|瞿曇悉達多|Kudon Shiddata|10=くどんしっだった}}, in his aspect as a [[Tathāgata]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[:en:Nirmanakaya|Nirmanakaya]] or [[:en:Sambhogakaya|Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the [[:en:Siddham|Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] since its origin in [[:en:India|India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=|1=Kaniman-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|1=Kaniman-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=|1=Kaniman-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|1=Seiryō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534101}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏|1=Reclining Buddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921452}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story and Ritual Bowl|qq=|1=Buddhist statue in Tōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119607}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=|1=Bodh Gaya Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644536}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * {{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Shakyamuni Tathāgata (釈迦如来) | sanskrit_name = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathāgata
Shakyamuni Tathāgata World Honored One
Shakyamuni Tathāgata
Muni
Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | image = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata statue ([[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]) | 経典 = [[:en:Lotus Sutra|Lotus Sutra]] | 信仰 = [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]]
[[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]] | 浄土 = [[:en:Vulture Peak|Vulture Peak]] Pure Land | See Also = [[:en:Prabhutaratna|Prabhutaratna Buddha]]
[[:en:Manjushri|Manjushri]]
[[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shakyamuni Tathāgata'''|釈迦如来|Shaka Nyorai}} refers to the historical [[:en:The Buddha|Buddha]] and founder of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], {{nihongo|'''Siddhartha Gautama'''|瞿曇悉達多|Kudon Shiddata|10=くどんしっだった}}, in his aspect as a [[Tathāgata]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[:en:Nirmanakaya|Nirmanakaya]] or [[:en:Sambhogakaya|Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the [[:en:Siddham|Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] since its origin in [[:en:India|India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|1=Seiryō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534101}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏|1=Reclining Buddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921452}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|qq=|1=Buddhist statue in Tōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119607}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=|1=Bodh Gaya Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644536}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|qq=|1=Sanzou Houshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866762}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the [[:en:Siddham|Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] since its origin in [[:en:India|India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|1=Seiryō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534101}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏|1=Reclining Buddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921452}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|qq=|1=Buddhist statue in Tōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119607}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=|1=Bodh Gaya Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644536}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|qq=|1=Sanzou Houshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866762}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|qq=|1=Sanbōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355123}} * [[:en:Śarīra|Śarīra]] == References == {{buddhism}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] [[Category:Buddhas]] [[Category:Gautama Buddha]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | image = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata statue ([[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]) | 経典 = [[:en:Lotus Sutra|Lotus Sutra]] | 信仰 = [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]]
[[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]] | 浄土 = [[:en:Vulture Peak|Vulture Peak]] Pure Land | See Also = [[:en:Prabhutaratna|Prabhutaratna Buddha]]

[[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shakyamuni Tathāgata'''|釈迦如来|Shaka Nyorai}} refers to the historical [[:en:The Buddha|Buddha]] and founder of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], {{nihongo|'''Siddhartha Gautama'''|瞿曇悉達多|Kudon Shiddata|10=くどんしっだった}}, in his aspect as a [[Tathāgata]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[:en:Nirmanakaya|Nirmanakaya]] or [[:en:Sambhogakaya|Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the [[:en:Siddham|Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] since its origin in [[:en:India|India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|qq=|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|1=Seiryō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534101}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏|1=Reclining Buddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921452}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|qq=|1=Buddhist statue in Tōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119607}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|qq=|1=Bodh Gaya Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644536}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|qq=|1=Sanzou Houshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866762}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner [[de:Shakyamuni]][[en:Shakyamuni]][[es:Shakyamuni]][[fr:Shakyamuni]][[ja:釈迦如来]][[pt:Shakyamuni]][[simple:Shakyamuni]][[zh:释迦如来]] {{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Shakyamuni Tathāgata (釈迦如来) | sanskrit_name = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathāgata
Shakyamuni Tathāgata World Honored One
Shakyamuni Tathāgata
Muni
Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | image = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata statue ([[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]) | 経典 = [[:en:Lotus Sutra|Lotus Sutra]] | 信仰 = [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]]
[[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]] | 浄土 = [[:en:Vulture Peak|Vulture Peak]] Pure Land | See Also = [[:en:Prabhutaratna|Prabhutaratna Buddha]]
[[:en:Manjushri|Manjushri]]
[[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shakyamuni Tathāgata'''|釈迦如来|Shaka Nyorai}} refers to the historical [[:en:The Buddha|Buddha]] and founder of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], {{nihongo|'''Siddhartha Gautama'''|瞿曇悉達多|Kudon Shiddata|10=くどんしっだった}}, in his aspect as a [[Tathāgata]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[:en:Nirmanakaya|Nirmanakaya]] or [[:en:Sambhogakaya|Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the [[:en:Siddham|Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] since its origin in [[:en:India|India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Seiryō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534101}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏|1=Reclining Buddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921452}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|1=Buddhist statue in Tōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119607}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|1=Bodh Gaya Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644536}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|1=Sanzou Houshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866762}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|1=Sanbōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355123}} * [[:en:Śarīra|Śarīra]] == References == {{buddhism}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] [[Category:Buddhas]] [[Category:Gautama Buddha]] {{wikidata link|Q11644537}}

Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | image = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata statue ([[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]) | 経典 = [[:en:Lotus Sutra|Lotus Sutra]] | 信仰 = [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]]
[[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]] | 浄土 = [[:en:Vulture Peak|Vulture Peak]] Pure Land | See Also = [[:en:Prabhutaratna|Prabhutaratna Buddha]]
[[:en:Manjushri|Manjushri]]
[[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shakyamuni Tathāgata'''|釈迦如来|Shaka Nyorai}} refers to the historical [[:en:The Buddha|Buddha]] and founder of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], {{nihongo|'''Siddhartha Gautama'''|瞿曇悉達多|Kudon Shiddata|10=くどんしっだった}}, in his aspect as a [[Tathāgata]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[:en:Nirmanakaya|Nirmanakaya]] or [[:en:Sambhogakaya|Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the [[:en:Siddham|Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] since its origin in [[:en:India|India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Seiryō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534101}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏|1=Reclining Buddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921452}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|1=Buddhist statue in Tōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119607}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|1=Bodh Gaya Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644536}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|1=Sanzou Houshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866762}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|1=Sanbōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355123}} * Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|1=Sanbōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355123}} *
