[[de:Shakyamuni]][[en:Shakyamuni]][[es:Shakyamuni]][[fr:Shakyamuni]][[ja:釈迦如来]][[pt:Shakyamuni]][[simple:Shakyamuni]][[zh:释迦如来]] {{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Shakyamuni Tathāgata (釈迦如来) | sanskrit_name = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathāgata
Shakyamuni Tathāgata World Honored One
Shakyamuni Tathāgata
Muni
Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | image = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata statue ([[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]) | 経典 = [[:en:Lotus Sutra|Lotus Sutra]] | 信仰 = [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]]
[[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]] | 浄土 = [[:en:Vulture Peak|Vulture Peak]] Pure Land | See Also = [[:en:Prabhutaratna|Prabhutaratna Buddha]]
[[:en:Manjushri|Manjushri]]
[[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shakyamuni Tathāgata'''|釈迦如来|Shaka Nyorai}} refers to the historical [[:en:The Buddha|Buddha]] and founder of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], {{nihongo|'''Siddhartha Gautama'''|瞿曇悉達多|Kudon Shiddata|10=くどんしっだった}}, in his aspect as a [[Tathāgata]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[:en:Nirmanakaya|Nirmanakaya]] or [[:en:Sambhogakaya|Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the [[:en:Siddham|Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] since its origin in [[:en:India|India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Seiryō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534101}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏|1=Reclining Buddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921452}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|1=Buddhist statue in Tōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119607}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|1=Bodh Gaya Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644536}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|1=Sanzou Houshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866762}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|1=Sanbōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355123}} * [[:en:Śarīra|Śarīra]] == References == {{buddhism}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[de:Shakyamuni]][[en:Shakyamuni]][[es:Shakyamuni]][[fr:Shakyamuni]][[ja:釈迦如来]][[pt:Shakyamuni]][[simple:Shakyamuni]][[zh:释迦如来]] {{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Shakyamuni Tathāgata (釈迦如来) | sanskrit_name = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathāgata
Shakyamuni Tathāgata World Honored One
Shakyamuni Tathāgata
Muni
Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | image = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata statue ([[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]) | 経典 = [[:en:Lotus Sutra|Lotus Sutra]] | 信仰 = [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]]
[[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]] | 浄土 = [[:en:Vulture Peak|Vulture Peak]] Pure Land | See Also = [[:en:Prabhutaratna|Prabhutaratna Buddha]]
[[:en:Manjushri|Manjushri]]
[[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shakyamuni Tathāgata'''|釈迦如来|Shaka Nyorai}} refers to the historical [[:en:The Buddha|Buddha]] and founder of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], {{nihongo|'''Siddhartha Gautama'''|瞿曇悉達多|Kudon Shiddata|10=くどんしっだった}}, in his aspect as a [[Tathāgata]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[:en:Nirmanakaya|Nirmanakaya]] or [[:en:Sambhogakaya|Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the [[:en:Siddham|Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] since its origin in [[:en:India|India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Seiryō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534101}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏|1=Reclining Buddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921452}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|1=Buddhist statue in Tōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119607}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|1=Bodh Gaya Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644536}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|1=Sanzou Houshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866762}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|1=Sanbōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355123}} * [[:en:Śarīra|Śarīra]] == References == {{buddhism}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] [[Category:Buddhas]] [[Category:Gautama Buddha]] {{wikidata link|Q11644537}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|1=Bodh Gaya Temple|comment=changed title based on Houshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866762}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of [[de:Shakyamuni]][[en:Shakyamuni]][[es:Shakyamuni]][[fr:Shakyamuni]][[ja:釈迦如来]][[pt:Shakyamuni]][[simple:Shakyamuni]][[zh:释迦如来]] [[de:Shakyamuni]][[en:Shakyamuni]][[es:Shakyamuni]][[fr:Shakyamuni]][[ja:釈迦如来]][[pt:Shakyamuni]][[simple:Shakyamuni]][[zh:释迦如来]] [[de:Shakyamuni]][[en:Shakyamuni]][[es:Shakyamuni]][[fr:Shakyamuni]][[ja:釈迦如来]][[pt:Shakyamuni]][[simple:Shakyamuni]][[zh:释迦如来]] {{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Shakyamuni Tathāgata (釈迦如来) | sanskrit_name = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathāgata
Shakyamuni Tathāgata World Honored One
Shakyamuni Tathāgata

Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | image = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]] wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata statue ([[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]) | 経典 = [[:en:Lotus Sutra|Lotus Sutra]] | 信仰 = [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]]
[[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]] | 浄土 = [[:en:Vulture Peak|Vulture Peak]] Pure Land | See Also = [[:en:Prabhutaratna|Prabhutaratna Buddha]]
[[:en:Manjushri|Manjushri]]
[[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shakyamuni Tathāgata'''|釈迦如来|Shaka Nyorai}} refers to the historical [[:en:The Buddha|Buddha]] and founder of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], {{nihongo|'''Siddhartha Gautama'''|瞿曇悉達多|Kudon Shiddata|10=くどんしっだった}}, in his aspect as a [[Tathāgata]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[:en:Nirmanakaya|Nirmanakaya]] or [[:en:Sambhogakaya|Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the [[:en:Siddham|Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] since its origin in [[:en:India|India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Seiryō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534101}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏|1=Reclining Buddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921452}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|1=Buddhist statue in Tōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119607}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|1=Bodh Gaya Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644536}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|1=Sanzou Houshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866762}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of [[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] }} {{nihongo|'''Shakyamuni Tathāgata'''|釈迦如来|Shaka Nyorai}} refers to the historical [[:en:The Buddha|Buddha]] and founder of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], {{nihongo|'''Siddhartha Gautama'''|瞿曇悉達多|Kudon Shiddata|10=くどんしっだった}}, in his aspect as a [[Tathāgata]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In [[:en:Theravada|Theravada Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In [[:en:Mahayana|Mahayana Buddhism]], Shakyamuni Tathāgata is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[:en:Nirmanakaya|Nirmanakaya]] or [[:en:Sambhogakaya|Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘[[:en:Jataka tales|Jataka tales]]’) : A sage under [[:en:Dipankara|Dipankara Buddha]], prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘[[:en:Lalitavistara Sūtra|Lalitavistara Sutra]]’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the [[:en:Tushita|Tusita Heaven]], considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Mahasthamaprapta]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the [[:en:Siddham|Siddham]] script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] since its origin in [[:en:India|India]]. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from [[:en:Hōryū-ji|Horyuji Temple]]'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, and [[:en:Murō-ji|Muroji Temple]]. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Seiryō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534101}} ([[:en:Seiryō-ji|Seiryoji Temple]], Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏|1=Reclining Buddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921452}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|1=Buddhist statue in Tōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119607}} ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Todaiji Temple]], Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like [[:en:Sensō-ji|Sensoji Temple]], [[:en:Dōjō-ji|Dojoji Temple]], [[:en:Nittai-ji|Kakuozan Nittai-ji]], and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|1=Bodh Gaya Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644536}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * [[:en:Journey to the West|Journey to the West]]: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|1=Sanzou Houshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866762}} and disciplines [[:en:Sun Wukong|Sun Wukong]]. * "The Spider's Thread" by [[:en:Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|Ryunosuke Akutagawa]]: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|1=Sanbōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355123}} * revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[de:Shakyamuni]][[en:Shakyamuni]][[es:Shakyamuni]][[fr:Shakyamuni]][[ja:釈迦如来]][[pt:Shakyamuni]][[simple:Shakyamuni]][[zh:释迦如来]] [[de:Shakyamuni]][[en:Shakyamuni]][[es:Shakyamuni]][[fr:Shakyamuni]][[ja:釈迦如来]][[pt:Shakyamuni]][[simple:Shakyamuni]][[zh:释迦如来]] [[de:Shakyamuni]][[en:Shakyamuni]][[es:Shakyamuni]][[fr:Shakyamuni]][[ja:釈迦如来]][[pt:Shakyamuni]][[simple:Shakyamuni]][[zh:释迦如来]] {{Citation-needed|date=September 26, 2017}} {{統合文字|迦}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Shakyamuni Tathāgata (釈迦如来) | sanskrit_name = शाक्यमुनि | 別名 = Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha
Shakyamuni Tathāgata
Shakyamuni Tathāgata World Honored One
Shakyamuni Tathāgata
Muni
Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | image = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}} wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata statue ({{ill|Murō-ji|en|Murō-ji|WD=Q1088506|lt=Muroji Temple}}) | 経典 = {{ill|Lotus Sutra|en|Lotus Sutra|WD=Q861212|lt=Lotus Sutra}} | 信仰 = {{ill|Theravada|en|Theravada|WD=Q132265|lt=Theravada Buddhism}}
{{ill|Mahayana|en|Mahayana|WD=Q48362|lt=Mahayana Buddhism}} | 浄土 = {{ill|Vulture Peak|en|Vulture Peak|WD=Q19755572|lt=Vulture Peak}} Pure Land | See Also = {{ill|Prabhutaratna|en|Prabhutaratna|WD=Q1815876|lt=Prabhutaratna Buddha}}
{{ill|Manjushri|en|Manjushri|WD=Q471696|lt=Manjushri}}
{{ill|Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|en|Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|WD=Q868306|lt=Samantabhadra}} }} {{nihongo|'''Shakyamuni Tathāgata'''|釈迦如来|Shaka Nyorai}} refers to the historical {{ill|The Buddha|en|The Buddha|WD=Q9441|lt=Buddha}} and founder of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}}, {{nihongo|'''Siddhartha Gautama'''|瞿曇悉達多|Kudon Shiddata|10=くどんしっだった}}, in his aspect as a [[Tathāgata]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada|en|Theravada|WD=Q132265|lt=Theravada Buddhism}}, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana|en|Mahayana|WD=Q48362|lt=Mahayana Buddhism}}, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[:en:Nirmanakaya|Nirmanakaya]] or [[:en:Sambhogakaya|Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|en|Jataka tales|WD=Q668439|lt=Jataka tales}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara|en|Dipankara|WD=Q1227131|lt=Dipankara Buddha}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sūtra|en|Lalitavistara Sūtra|WD=Q1381826|lt=Lalitavistara Sutra}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tushita|en|Tushita|WD=Q660582|lt=Tusita Heaven}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|en|Mahasthamaprapta|WD=Q1465683|lt=Mahasthamaprapta}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the {{ill|Siddham|en|Siddham|WD=Q47016004|lt=Siddham}} script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}} since its origin in {{ill|India|en|India|WD=Q668|lt=India}}. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Horyuji Temple}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, and {{ill|Murō-ji|en|Murō-ji|WD=Q1088506|lt=Muroji Temple}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Seiryō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534101}} ({{ill|Seiryō-ji|en|Seiryō-ji|WD=Q534101|lt=Seiryoji Temple}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏|1=Reclining Buddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921452}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|1=Buddhist statue in Tōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119607}} ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Todaiji Temple}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like {{ill|Sensō-ji|en|Sensō-ji|WD=Q615183|lt=Sensoji Temple}}, {{ill|Dōjō-ji|en|Dōjō-ji|WD=Q1272708|lt=Dojoji Temple}}, {{ill|Nittai-ji|en|Nittai-ji|WD=Q3342235|lt=Kakuozan Nittai-ji}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|1=Bodh Gaya Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644536}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|en|Journey to the West|WD=Q70784|lt=Journey to the West}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|1=Sanzou Houshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866762}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|en|Sun Wukong|WD=Q11773777|lt=Sun Wukong}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|en|Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|WD=Q186326|lt=Ryunosuke Akutagawa}}: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Concepts == * {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|ja|釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|1=Three Sages of Avatamsaka|2=ja|3=釈迦三尊|4=zh|5=華嚴三聖|6=commons|7=Category:Three Sages of Avatamsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} * {{ill|Triple Gem|ja|三宝尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Gem|1=Sanbōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355123}} * {{ill|Śarīra|en|Śarīra|WD=Q1156436|lt=Śarīra}} == References == {{buddhism}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦如来|version=104339140|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:釈迦如来]] [[ja:Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] {{wikidata link|Q11644537}} [[Category:Buddhas]] {{ill|Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|en|Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|WD=Q868306|lt=Samantabhadra}} }} {{nihongo|'''Shakyamuni Tathāgata'''|釈迦如来|Shaka Nyorai}} refers to the historical {{ill|The Buddha|en|The Buddha|WD=Q9441|lt=Buddha}} and founder of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}}, {{nihongo|'''Siddhartha Gautama'''|瞿曇悉達多|Kudon Shiddata|10=くどんしっだった}}, in his aspect as a [[Tathāgata]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada|en|Theravada|WD=Q132265|lt=Theravada Buddhism}}, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana|en|Mahayana|WD=Q48362|lt=Mahayana Buddhism}}, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[:en:Nirmanakaya|Nirmanakaya]] or [[:en:Sambhogakaya|Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|en|Jataka tales|WD=Q668439|lt=Jataka tales}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara|en|Dipankara|WD=Q1227131|lt=Dipankara Buddha}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sūtra|en|Lalitavistara Sūtra|WD=Q1381826|lt=Lalitavistara Sutra}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tushita|en|Tushita|WD=Q660582|lt=Tusita Heaven}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|en|Mahasthamaprapta|WD=Q1465683|lt=Mahasthamaprapta}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the {{ill|Siddham|en|Siddham|WD=Q47016004|lt=Siddham}} script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}} since its origin in {{ill|India|en|India|WD=Q668|lt=India}}. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Horyuji Temple}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, and {{ill|Murō-ji|en|Murō-ji|WD=Q1088506|lt=Muroji Temple}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|en|Jataka tales|WD=Q668439|lt=Jataka tales}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara|en|Dipankara|WD=Q1227131|lt=Dipankara Buddha}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sūtra|en|Lalitavistara Sūtra|WD=Q1381826|lt=Lalitavistara Sutra}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tushita|en|Tushita|WD=Q660582|lt=Tusita Heaven}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|en|Mahasthamaprapta|WD=Q1465683|lt=Mahasthamaprapta}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the {{ill|Siddham|en|Siddham|WD=Q47016004|lt=Siddham}} script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}} since its origin in {{ill|India|en|India|WD=Q668|lt=India}}. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Horyuji Temple}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, and {{ill|Murō-ji|en|Murō-ji|WD=Q1088506|lt=Muroji Temple}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures * {{ill|Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|蟹満寺#文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}} ({{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|清凉寺#木造釈迦如来立像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Standing Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=Seiryō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534101}} ({{ill|Seiryō-ji|en|Seiryō-ji|WD=Q534101|lt=Seiryoji Temple}}, Kyoto) * {{ill|Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|涅槃像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reclining Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 涅槃仏|1=Reclining Buddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2921452}} ({{ill|Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja|法隆寺五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horyuji Temple Five-Story Pagoda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|ja|東大寺の仏像#銅造誕生釈迦仏立像及び灌仏盤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Birth Shakyamuni Tathāgata and Ritual Bowl|1=Buddhist statue in Tōdai-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119607}} ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Todaiji Temple}}, Nara) == Relic Veneration == Japan houses relics of Shakyamuni Tathāgata in temples like {{ill|Sensō-ji|en|Sensō-ji|WD=Q615183|lt=Sensoji Temple}}, {{ill|Dōjō-ji|en|Dōjō-ji|WD=Q1272708|lt=Dojoji Temple}}, {{ill|Nittai-ji|en|Nittai-ji|WD=Q3342235|lt=Kakuozan Nittai-ji}}, and {{ill|Bodh Gaya Temple|ja|釈迦山大菩提寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodh Gaya Temple|1=Bodh Gaya Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644536}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Fiction == * {{ill|Journey to the West|en|Journey to the West|WD=Q70784|lt=Journey to the West}}: Guides {{ill|Tang Sanzang|ja|三蔵法師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang Sanzang|1=Sanzou Houshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866762}} and disciplines {{ill|Sun Wukong|en|Sun Wukong|WD=Q11773777|lt=Sun Wukong}}. * "The Spider's Thread" by {{ill|Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|en|Ryūnosuke Akutagawa|WD=Q186326|lt=Ryunosuke Akutagawa}}: Shakyamuni Tathāgata grants redemption to the sinner Kandata. == Related Shakya World Honored One | 種字 = [[File:Bhah.svg|25px]] Bak | 真言・陀羅尼 = Nōmaku Sanmanda Bodanan Bak | image = [[File:Shaka Muroji Mirokudo.jpg|250px]] | 説明文 = {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} wooden seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata statue ({{ill|Murō-ji|en|Murō-ji|WD=Q1088506|lt=Muroji Temple|1=Murō-ji}}) | 経典 = {{ill|Lotus Sutra|en|Lotus Sutra|WD=Q861212|lt=Lotus Sutra|1=Lotus Sutra}} | 信仰 = {{ill|Theravada|en|Theravada|WD=Q132265|lt=Theravada Buddhism|1=Theravada}}
{{ill|Mahayana|en|Mahayana|WD=Q48362|lt=Mahayana Buddhism|1=Mahayana}} | 浄土 = {{ill|Vulture Peak|en|Vulture Peak|WD=Q19755572|lt=Vulture Peak|1=Vulture Peak}} Pure Land | See Also = {{ill|Prabhutaratna|en|Prabhutaratna|WD=Q1815876|lt=Prabhutaratna Buddha|1=Prabhutaratna}}
{{ill|Manjushri|en|Manjushri|WD=Q471696|lt=Manjushri|1=Manjushri}}
{{ill|Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|en|Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|WD=Q868306|lt=Samantabhadra|1=Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)}} }} {{nihongo|'''Shakyamuni Tathāgata'''|釈迦如来|Shaka Nyorai}} refers to the historical {{ill|The Buddha|en|The Buddha|WD=Q9441|lt=Buddha|1=The Buddha}} and founder of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}}, {{nihongo|'''Siddhartha Gautama'''|瞿曇悉達多|Kudon Shiddata|10=くどんしっだった}}, in his aspect as a [[Tathāgata]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata in Buddhist History == === Theravada Buddhism === In {{ill|Theravada|en|Theravada|WD=Q132265|lt=Theravada Buddhism|1=Theravada}}, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is regarded as a historical and living Buddha, a manifestation distinct from the conceptualized [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]].[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%90%86%E4%BB%8F%E6%80%A7-658308 ‘理仏性’ - Kotobank] === Mahayana Buddhism === In {{ill|Mahayana|en|Mahayana|WD=Q48362|lt=Mahayana Buddhism|1=Mahayana}}, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is seen as an emanation of the [[:en:Dharmakaya|Dharmakaya]] Buddha, teaching the Dharma in the form of a [[:en:Nirmanakaya|Nirmanakaya]] or [[:en:Sambhogakaya|Sambhogakaya]]. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata's Past Lives == ; Sumedha (from ‘{{ill|Buddhavamsa|ja|仏種生経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhavamsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ and ‘{{ill|Jataka tales|en|Jataka tales|WD=Q668439|lt=Jataka tales|1=Jataka tales}}’) : A sage under {{ill|Dipankara|en|Dipankara|WD=Q1227131|lt=Dipankara Buddha|1=Dipankara}}, prophesied to become a Buddha in the future. ; Jyotipala Bodhisattva (‘{{ill|Lalitavistara Sūtra|en|Lalitavistara Sūtra|WD=Q1381826|lt=Lalitavistara Sutra|1=Lalitavistara Sūtra}}’) : Sumedha's reincarnation in the {{ill|Tushita|en|Tushita|WD=Q660582|lt=Tusita Heaven|1=Tushita}}, considered a previous incarnation of Shakyamuni Tathāgata. ; Nagabhadra (‘{{ill|Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja|観世音菩薩往生浄土本縁経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokitesvara Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’) : A Brahmin priest whose children became {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|1=Avalokiteśvara}} and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|en|Mahasthamaprapta|WD=Q1465683|lt=Mahasthamaprapta|1=Mahasthamaprapta}}. == Shakyamuni Tathāgata as Depicted in Statues == [[File:Bhah.svg|thumb|Shakyamuni Tathāgata represented by the {{ill|Siddham|en|Siddham|WD=Q47016004|lt=Siddham|1=Siddham}} script seed syllable]] Statues of Shakyamuni Tathāgata have been created throughout regions influenced by {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}} since its origin in {{ill|India|en|India|WD=Q668|lt=India|1=India}}. Depictions often narrate his life through artistic interpretations of various scenes, such as his birth, asceticism, enlightenment, teaching, and parinirvana. In Japan, Shakyamuni Tathāgata is often depicted in seated teaching postures (Dharmachakra Mudra), signifying his role in spreading the Dharma. Common examples include statues from {{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Horyuji Temple|1=Hōryū-ji}}'s Golden Hall, {{ill|Kanimanji Temple|ja|蟹満寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanimanji Temple|1=Kaniman-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625884}}, and {{ill|Murō-ji|en|Murō-ji|WD=Q1088506|lt=Muroji Temple|1=Murō-ji}}. === Notable Statues === ; National Treasures *
